name: Masadas y Casas de San Juan — circular desde Cantavieja (BTT06 Maestrazgo)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cantavieja offers diverse landscapes within the Maestrazgo region of Teruel, Spain. The area is characterized by rugged terrain, limestone rock formations, and the Iberian Mountain Range, providing a challenging environment for cyclists. Its strategic location features ravines, mountains, and quiet roads with minimal traffic, making it suitable for various mountain bike trails. The region is part of the Maestrazgo Geopark, highlighting its natural value and varied territory. Best mountain bike trails around Cantavieja The most popular mountain bike trail is Masadas y Casas de San Juan — circular desde Cantavieja (BTT06 Maestrazgo), a 20.3 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural surroundings of Cantavieja. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Barranco de la Tosquilla — circular desde Iglesuela del Cid (BTT05 Maestrazgo), a moderate 13.4 miles (21.6 km) path. This trail offers scenic views through the Barranco de la Tosquilla. Local mountain bikers also love the Tower of La Iglesuela del Cid – Iglesuela del Cid loop from La Iglesuela del Cid, a 24.4 miles (39.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain around La Iglesuela del Cid, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around Cantavieja is defined by rugged relief, limestone rock formations, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cantavieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cantavieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cantavieja?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Cantavieja listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Maestrazgo region.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly mountain bike routes in Cantavieja?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its rugged terrain, there is at least one easy mountain bike route available. The Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center also offers routes catering to all skill levels, including green (easy) routes.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Cantavieja?
A: Absolutely. Cantavieja and the surrounding Maestrazgo region are ideal for advanced riders. There are 12 difficult mountain bike trails listed on komoot, featuring rugged relief, significant elevation changes, and mountain passes with gradients up to 19%. The challenging Maestrazgo Loop, over 200 kilometers long with more than 4,600 meters of elevation gain, is a prime example for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike trails around Cantavieja?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995599051/masadas-y-casas-de-san-juan-circular-desde-cantavieja-btt06-maestrazgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masadas y Casas de San Juan — circular desde Cantavieja (BTT06 Maestrazgo)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995598493/barranco-de-la-tosquilla-circular-desde-iglesuela-del-cid-btt05-maestrazgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Tosquilla — circular desde Iglesuela del Cid (BTT05 Maestrazgo)&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views on a circular path.

Q: What kind of landscapes and views can I expect along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Cantavieja offer diverse and striking landscapes within the Maestrazgo region. You'll encounter rugged terrain, limestone rock formations, deep ravines, and mountains within the Iberian Mountain Range. Cantavieja itself is perched on a limestone rock at 1300 meters, providing impressive views. The area is part of the Maestrazgo Geopark, highlighting its significant natural beauty and varied territory.

Q: What are some interesting places or landmarks to see near the mountain bike routes?
A: The region is rich in cultural and natural heritage. You can explore the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4826891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantavieja&lt;/a&gt; itself, a Historic-Artistic Site. Other nearby settlements worth visiting include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5227100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesuela del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortanete Village&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirambel&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Maestrazgo region is also home to four Natural Monuments and is part of the Maestrazgo Cultural Park.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cantavieja?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cantavieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, often praising the rugged relief and diverse landscapes.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Cantavieja?
A: Yes, the Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center, which serves the region, offers services including parking for cyclists. Cantavieja is also a recommended starting point for many routes, suggesting parking facilities are available for visitors.

Q: Are there any specific facilities for mountain bikers in the area?
A: The Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center provides comprehensive services for cyclists, including information points, bike repair and rental, parking, washing points, showers, and toilets. This makes it a convenient base for exploring the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cantavieja?
A: The region's mountain passes can reach heights of 1,500-1,700 meters, which means conditions can vary significantly with seasons. While specific advice on the best time isn't provided, the rugged terrain and elevation suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or potential winter snow and ice at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes that connect through Cantavieja?
A: Yes, Cantavieja is a recommended starting point for the challenging Maestrazgo Loop, a circular MTB route spanning over 200 kilometers with more than 4,600 meters of positive elevation gain. This route primarily consists of off-road sections and connects with other significant routes like Montañas Vacías, leading through remote and captivating areas of Teruel.
tips: Cantavieja is another wonderful town belonging to the Teruel Maestrazgo that will encourage us to get lost in its ancient and beautiful streets and corners until we reach the town's castle. Without a doubt, the town of Cantavieja deserves to be listed among the 100 most beautiful towns in Spain.

Las Casas de San Juan is one of the farmer neighborhoods that were part of the municipality of Cantavieja. Currently abandoned, we find it in the ravine of the Cuba river and we will also see it if we take route 6 of the Maestrazgo btt center.
